preamble: The elision of [z] is a common stage of sound weakening, often following a debuccalization path. In Romance languages, intervocalic and syllable-final voiced sibilants frequently disappeared, leaving traces in the vowel length or orthography.

examples.note: Historical loss of the medial sibilant.
Common elision of syllable-final sibilants in Caribbean and Andalusian Spanish.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
